mappings.rationaleAI: Four-wheeled motor vehicles for passenger transport correspond to automobiles (C.4) and the development of road vehicles (A.5.a).
Machines designed for self-propulsion to transport people or cargo correspond to vehicles for road transportation (C), types of ships (F.1), and aircraft (G.1).

Intentional movement of humans and goods via all modes corresponds comprehensively to the history of transportation (A), roads and highways (B), road vehicles (C), rail (D), water (F), and air transportation (G).
Design and construction of roads, bridges, and railways corresponds to roads and highways construction (B) and the development of the vehicle wheel and roads (A.2).
Coupled vehicles on railway tracks propelled by locomotives correspond to rail transportation (D) and the development of the first locomotives and spread of railways (A.4.a).
Engineered waterways for transport correspond to canals and inland waterways (F.3) and the historical development of inland waterways (A.3).
Vehicles achieving flight correspond to aircraft configurations (G.1), lighter-than-air craft (G.1.a), and heavier-than-air craft including fixed-wing and rotary-wing (G.1.b).
Steam power and railways correspond to steam transportation (A.4), railroad development (A.4.a), and steam navigation (A.4.b).
ICEs as the dominant power source for vehicles correspond to development of road vehicles (A.5.a) and automobiles (C.4).
Large watercraft for ocean transit correspond to water transportation (F), types of ships (F.1), and ship design and construction (F.2).
Energy conversion to mechanical motion corresponds to power units for ship propulsion including steam, ICE, gas turbines, and nuclear reactors (F.2.c).
Steam engines driving locomotives and steamships correspond to steam transportation (A.4), railroads (A.4.a), and steam navigation (A.4.b).
Human-powered pedal-driven two-wheeled vehicles correspond to bicycles (C.3).
Thoroughfares for vehicular traffic correspond to roads and highways and their construction (B) and development of roads (A.2).
Radio wave detection for air traffic control and navigation connects to aeronautical research (G.5).
Pioneered mass automobile production making car ownership accessible, corresponding to automobiles (C.4) and construction of road vehicles (A.5.a).
The wheel and axle as foundational to transportation corresponds to the wheel and the road (A.2).
Self-propelled vehicles for space travel correspond to space travel (G.4).
Civil engineering structures for transportation correspond to construction of railroad bridges and tunnels (A.4.a) and construction of bridges (A.5.a).
Monitoring and controlling vehicle movement across marine, land, and air domains connects to water transportation (F) and navigational systems.
Electric motors powering electric vehicles connect to automobiles (C.4) as electric vehicles rapidly grow in share.
Traces transportation technology from primitive forms through steam, combustion, and modern developments, corresponding to the history of transportation (A).
Physical systems including roads and railways correspond to roads and highways and their construction (B).
Oceans as the medium for maritime trade and navigation connect to water transportation (F).
Electronic systems in aircraft including avionics connect to aeronautical research (G.5).
